Package org.eventb.core.tests.pom

Class AdversarialReasoner

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.core.tests.pom.AdversarialReasoner
All Implemented Interfaces:
IReasoner, IRepairableInputReasoner
public class AdversarialReasoner extends Object implements IReasoner, IRepairableInputReasoner
AdversarialReasoner reasoner and tactic that can fail badly (e.g. throw any exception). This class is used to ensure that the sequent prover is resilient to errors in contributed extensions.

    • getReasonerID

      public String getReasonerID()
      Description copied from interface: IReasoner
      Returns the unique id of this reasoner, that is the value of the id field used when registering it to the extension point org.eventb.core.seqprover.reasoners.
      Specified by:
      getReasonerID in interface IReasoner
      Returns:
      the unique id of this reasoner

    • serializeInput

      public void serializeInput(IReasonerInput input, IReasonerInputWriter writer) throws SerializeException
      Description copied from interface: IReasoner
      Serializes the given input using the given writer.

      Clients should store with the given writer all data of the input that can not be retrieved from the proof rule that was generated by the last successful application of this reasoner to a sequent.

      Specified by:
      serializeInput in interface IReasoner
      Parameters:
      input - the input to serialize
      writer - the writer to use
      Throws:
      SerializeException - In case of error when serializing.

    • deserializeInput

      public IReasonerInput deserializeInput(IReasonerInputReader reader) throws SerializeException
      Description copied from interface: IReasoner
      Deserializes an input of this reasoner using the given reader.

      Clients should retrieve from the reader all data needed to reconstruct an input that was previously serialized. The reader provides means to access to data serialized explicitly, as well as to the proof rule that was generated from the serialized input.

      The returned value must not be null.

      Specified by:
      deserializeInput in interface IReasoner
      Parameters:
      reader - the reader to use
      Returns:
      a new instance of the input that was previously serialized
      Throws:
      SerializeException - In case of error when serializing.

    • apply

      public IReasonerOutput apply(IProverSequent seq, IReasonerInput input, IProofMonitor pm)
      Description copied from interface: IReasoner
      Apply this reasoner to the given sequent with the given input.
      Specified by:
      apply in interface IReasoner
      Parameters:
      seq - the sequent to which this reasoner is applied
      input - the input to this reasoner
      pm - a proof monitor (provides cancellation)
      Returns:
      the result of applying this reasoner to the given sequent (either a proof rule or a failure)
      See Also:
      • IProofRule
      • IReasonerFailure

    • asTactic

      public ITactic asTactic()

    • repair

      public IReasonerInput repair(IReasonerInputReader reader)
      Description copied from interface: IRepairableInputReasoner
      Tries to repair an input after a SerializeException has occurred.
      Specified by:
      repair in interface IRepairableInputReasoner
      Parameters:
      reader - an input reader
      Returns:
      the repaired input, or null in case of failure
